subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Apply all resources from state file only when there are changes

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build all resources from state file

subcommands.name: cache
subcommands.description: Cache management

subcommands.name: charts
subcommands.description: DEPRECATED: sync releases from state file (helm upgrade --install)

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: The helmfile delete sub-command deletes all the releases defined in the manifests

subcommands.name: deps
subcommands.description: Update charts based on their requirements, it locks your helmfile state and local charts dependencies

subcommands.name: destroy
subcommands.description: Destroys and then purges releases

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Diff releases defined in state file, it executes the helm-diff plugin across all of the charts/releases defined in the manifest

subcommands.name: fetch
subcommands.description: Fetch charts from state file, it downloads or copies local charts to a local directory for debug purpose

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Help about any command

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize the helmfile, includes version checking and installation of helm and plug-ins

subcommands.name: lint
subcommands.description: Lint charts from state file (helm lint)

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List releases defined in state file

subcommands.name: repos
subcommands.description: Repos releases defined in state file

subcommands.name: secrets
subcommands.description: Causes the helm-secrets plugin to be executed to decrypt the file

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Retrieve status of releases in state file

subcommands.name: sync
subcommands.description: Sync releases defined in state file

subcommands.name: template
subcommands.description: Template releases defined in state file

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Test charts from state file (helm test)

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Print the CLI version

subcommands.name: write-values
subcommands.description: Write values files for releases. Similar to `helmfile template`, write values files instead of manifests

options.description: Do not exit with an error code if the provided selector has no matching releases
Set chart. Uses the chart set in release by default, and is available in template as {{ .Chart }}
Enable verbose output for Helm and set log-level to debug, this disables --quiet/-q effect

Show live output from the Helm binary Stdout/Stderr into Helmfile own Stdout/Stderr. It only applies for the Helm CLI commands, Stdout/Stderr for Hooks are still displayed only when it's execution finishes
Specify the environment name. defaults to default
Load config from file or directory. defaults to helmfile.yaml or `helmfile.d`(means `helmfile.d/*.yaml`) in this preference
Request confirmation before attempting to modify clusters
Set kubectl context. Uses current context by default
Set log level, default info (default info)
Set namespace. Uses the namespace set in the context by default, and is available in templates as {{ .Namespace }}
Silence output. Equivalent to log-level warn
Only run using the releases that match labels. Labels can take the form of foo=bar or foo!=bar
Specify state values in a YAML file
Set state values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)
